repo_prior_art.cite: eid_dml_orth_uniform_v1: DML-LRO-ADML studentization frontier, not fold-mass variance

published_prior_art.cite: ChernozhukovEtAl2018DML and BachEtAl2024DoubleMLR: equal fixed K-fold DML score inference
AhrensHansenSchafferWiemann2024DDML custom/repeated folds; ChiangKatoMaSasaki2022MultiwayDML geometry-specific robust SE, neither states a fold-mass sandwich
Velez2024DMLGrowingK: growing K separates DML1/DML2, not vanishing fold-mass DML2 report separation

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: Exhibit 9.2 specifies a nondegenerate ATE law but not a concrete fold/nuisance mechanism producing the advertised small-fold B_{2,n} perturbation and tau^2/lambda gap.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The two-fold report-separation witness is promised, but no Section 9 exhibit computes V_obs and V_fm from the stated law plus a concrete learner/split primitive.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The Velez2024DMLGrowingK comparator is used as the closest asymptotic-regime contrast without a section, proposition, equation, page, or quoted claim anchor.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: Under Assumption witness, B_{2,n}=xi_n/sqrt(n a_n) gives sqrt(n) a_n B_{2,n}=sqrt(a_n) xi_n -> 0 and n a_n^2 Var(B_{2,n})=a_n tau^2 -> 0, contradicting the claimed tau^2/lambda variance gap.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: Theorem 1 assumes Assumptions id--equal, but Assumption equal refers to the fold-linear term from Assumption foldlinear, which is not included in the theorem hypotheses.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Repair the small-fold scaling: state the B_{2,n} order that actually yields n a_n^2 Var(B_{2,n}) -> tau^2/lambda, then check it is compatible with nuisance-rate and overlap assumptions.
Replace Exhibit 9.2 with a full worked witness: observable law, deterministic fold sequence, concrete nuisance learner or perturbation model, computed S_k, V_obs, V_fm, and the internal rate check.
Add location-level anchors for Velez 2024 and the ddml custom-fold/repeated-cross-fitting claims, preferably section or equation references tied to the exact gap.

recommended_next_step: Reject v1 as internally inconsistent; redraft only after the small-fold perturbation scaling and concrete witness are rebuilt from primitives.
